question: Какие два специальных метода реализует класс FrenchDeck в главе 1?
expected_evidence: chunk that explicitly names class FrenchDeck together with the two methods it implements (__len__ and __getitem__), either via the class definition itself in code OR via prose that ties FrenchDeck to those two specific method names; partial evidence (only a len(deck) demo, or only one of the two methods) is candidate, not gold
forbidden_evidence: generic lists of dunder methods without FrenchDeck context (e.g., fluent_ch01_010)
Vector class definitions or method lists — different class, different methods (e.g., fluent_ch01_016, fluent_ch01_017)
FrenchDeck mentions that do not name both methods (chunk saying 'FrenchDeck is a sequence-like example' without specifying which two methods make it so)

demos of len(deck) or deck[0] without showing the underlying method definitions — these prove behavior, not implementation, and could be true via inheritance from a base class (e.g., fluent_ch01_004 alone)
any claim that FrenchDeck implements MORE than these two methods unless the chunk also lists the additional ones — book ch1 attributes only these two
audit_notes: Q-known-hit pilot. evidence_verdict=supported assigned on TYPED grounds, not because the system's answer matches benchmark gold (per Pack v0.2.1 Q-known-hit rule): chunk fluent_ch01_003 contains BOTH (a) the class FrenchDeck definition with two def lines `def __len__(self):` and `def __getitem__(self, position):`, AND (b) prose saying FrenchDeck implements these two methods. The relation (FrenchDeck implements X) and the specific method names co-occur in one chunk, so typed support is local to a single chunk. forbidden_evidence was drafted before inspecting retrieved chunks (per Pack 4.3): Vector chunks (ch01_016, ch01_017) and the generic dunder-list chunk (ch01_010) appear in top-k and would have been tempting decoys had the gold not been at rank 1. audit_confidence='medium', not 'high', for two structural reasons: (1) LLM single-rater (claude-opus-4-7), with bias profile distinct from human; (2) the rater also self-constructed the chunks_subset and the simulated run, so confirmation bias is structurally present even when each individual judgment is defensible. Pack v0.2.1 single-rater warning applies a fortiori to LLM-self-constructed test bed.
